title: Recruiter Agentic AI Depth Questions
summary: Recruiter-oriented question set for testing Joz's technical depth in agentic AI architecture and production AI systems.
body:
Recruiter Agentic AI Depth Questions

Purpose
This record covers recruiter questions that probe beyond generic "AI" language into actual architecture depth.

Question set
- How technical is Joz in AI?
- What kind of AI systems does Joz build?
- How strong is Joz in agentic AI architecture?
- Does Joz understand RAG, orchestration, memory, or evaluation?
- Is Joz hands-on or just high-level?
- Can Joz work across product, architecture, and implementation?

Answer guidance
- Describe the full agent operating model: intent, retrieval, authorised context, reasoning, planning, execution, verification, and human escalation.
- Make it clear Joz is not just a prompt engineer or generic AI consultant.
- Use enterprise retrieval, ACL-aware responses, observability, governance, and production workflow language when the recruiter asks for depth.
- Keep the explanation accessible to non-technical recruiters while still sounding technically real.

Proof anchors
- Agentic AI architecture, RAG, embeddings, vector search, knowledge graphs, memory, verification, and observability.
- MarketClue and current AI initiatives as live-product proof.
- Enterprise context engineering and governance framing from current Joz LLM architecture work.

verification.notes: Aligned with the existing verified agentic AI architecture records and current router answers.

claims: Joz can be positioned as hands-on in agentic AI architecture rather than only strategic.
The strongest technical framing is the full operating model of enterprise agents across retrieval, reasoning, execution, verification, and governance.
Recruiter answers should distinguish Joz from prompt-only AI profiles.
proof_points: Existing architecture records cover orchestration, RAG, embeddings, memory, ACL-aware retrieval, verification, and observability.
Current Joz LLM and MarketClue work provide live-system relevance.
